titleStmt.title: SII 32, part 2, no. 229: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 229. (A.R. No. 17 of 1920.) TIRUMAḺAVĀḌI, UDAIYARPALAIYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the west wall, central shrine, Vaidyanātha temple.
Beginning is damaged. It records a gift of 4 mā of land in two pieces after purchase for thrity kaḻañju of gold, for providing mid-night service (in the temple) by Pirāntakan Mādēvaḍigaḷ.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX No. 167.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
vaṭi tevan nilattukku teṟkkum Ivvicaitta perunānkellaiyilum makappaṭṭa nilam Oru māvum Ākat taṭi Ira
ṇṭināl nilam nānku māvum Innilam mikutikkuṟaimai Uḷḷaṭaṅka vilai koṇṭu kuṭutta poṉ muppatiṉ
kaḻañcu kuṭuttu Innilam nānku māvum vilaikoṇṭu Artta yāmattukkuk koṇṭu kuṭutten pirāntakan
mātevaṭikaḷen Itu panmāheśvara rakṣai .
An inscription attributed to Uttamachōḷa's period, is found engraved on the south wall mukhamaṇḍapa of Jambuliggasvāmi temple at ŚirukaṉpUr, Perambalur Taluk and district. The text runs as follows:
1. svastiśrī . i[ttiru]maṇ-
2. ṭapam uttama coḻa-
3. n tirumaṇṭapam . ṣee ṭamiḻnaṭṭuk kalveṭṭukaḷ Vol. III (2010) P. 104. But the inscription is yet to be critically examined.
Digital edition of SII 32, part 2, no. 229 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
